cot_prefix: Okay, let's look closely at the article to find the correct answer. The question is about the technologies used in Google Glass and whether they're completely new, mostly from smartphones, high-tech but outdated, or all old. The first paragraph of the article says, "Most of the technologies for Google Glass are already available on smart phones. Google has taken those same technologies and added them to eyeglass frames." So right away, the article is telling us that most of the technologies used in Google Glass are ones that are already in smartphones. The key phrase here is "most of the technologies... are already available on smartphones." The options are: A) completely new B) mostly already in use for smartphones C) high-tech but out-of-date D) all old The article specifically mentions that "most of the technologies" are already in smartphones, which
